content.claim: The January 2020 tightening of school-readiness assessment and deferral rules resulted in deferral applications falling sharply to 16,400 by 2021, and KSH time-series data show SNI numbers rising markedly faster from 2021/2022 onward.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: KSH itself has not issued an official causal attribution linking the policy change to SNI increases; the link is attributed to advocacy-group interpretation and journalistic synthesis
Multiple explanatory factors (diagnostic improvement, genuine prevalence change, assessment-access changes) operate simultaneously, and sources do not weight their relative contributions
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
